name: A Coruña
summary: A Coruña is a windy seaside city in Galicia, northwest Spain, where Atlantic waves crash on rocky cliffs. It is famous for the Tower of Hercules, a Roman lighthouse that has flashed its light to ships for almost 2,000 years.
funFacts: The Tower of Hercules is the world's oldest working lighthouse — Romans built it nearly 1,900 years ago and its light still warns ships every night.
A Coruña is nicknamed the 'Crystal City' because hundreds of houses have white glass balconies that sparkle in the sun and shelter people from rainy weather.
An old legend says the hero Hercules battled a giant called Geryon for three days here, then buried the giant's head and built a city on top.
The local treat is pulpo — boiled octopus snipped with scissors onto a wooden plate, sprinkled with paprika and salt, and eaten with toothpicks.
People in Galicia speak their own language called Galego, which sounds a bit like a mix of Spanish and Portuguese, and they say 'Ola' for hello.
thingsToDo: Climb the spiral staircase up the Tower of Hercules for huge views over the wild Atlantic Ocean.
Meet sharks, octopuses and a friendly seal at the Aquarium Finisterrae's touch pools and underwater Nautilus room.
Build sandcastles and paddle in the rolling waves at Riazor Beach right in the middle of the city.
Walk along the glass-galleried Marina to spot hundreds of sparkling white balconies above the seafront.
Cross the bridge to the Castle of San Antón, an old island fortress that is now a museum of pirates and treasures.
Wander Plaza de María Pita, the huge arcaded square named after a brave local heroine, and watch street musicians play.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Small black-and-white seabird with a stripy orange beak that nests on wild Atlantic cliffs.

wildlife.name: Common Octopus
wildlife.description: Eight-armed sea creature called pulpo here, very clever and Galicia's most famous dish.

wildlife.name: Gooseneck Barnacle
wildlife.description: Strange shellfish called percebes that cling to rocks battered by big crashing waves.

wildlife.name: Common Dolphin
wildlife.description: Sleek grey dolphin with yellow side stripes that leaps through waves off the Galician coast.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Galego (Galician), the language spoken in A Coruña.

images.alt: The ancient Tower of Hercules lighthouse glows at sunrise on a rocky headland above the Atlantic in A Coruña.
Plaza de María Pita's grand arcaded square sits in front of A Coruña's elegant city hall with its blue domes.
A long row of white glass-fronted balconies sparkles along A Coruña's Marina seafront, the city's famous galerías.
Riazor's wide golden sand beach curves along the city, with gentle Atlantic waves rolling in.
The seaside entrance to A Coruña's Aquarium Finisterrae beside a rocky bay.
The stone Castle of San Antón sits on its little island in A Coruña harbour.
An Atlantic puffin with a colourful orange beak stands on a grassy clifftop.
A common octopus stretches its eight long arms across a rocky seabed.
A cluster of gooseneck barnacles, called percebes, clings tightly to a wave-splashed rock.
A common dolphin leaps clear of the ocean, showing its yellow side stripe.
